label: Squarish 2BR / 2 baths CLUSTERED with mid-band HALL — bedrooms and the common T&B all open onto a hallway tucked into the middle band between master (front) and standard (rear). Hall has an open mouth east into the great_room. Open-plan great_room + kitchen.

adjacencies.note: ensuite is master's exclusive — interior door from master, west side of the bath block
shared plumbing wall — wet stacks in one bay
common's north face touches standard — bath block is the acoustic+privacy buffer between bedrooms
master door opens north into the hall — 1.0 m floor = 0.8 m clear + 2x 0.10 m frame stops
common bath door opens east into the hall — 0.8 m bath leaf + frame
standard door opens south into the hall — 1.0 m = 0.8 m clear + frame
hall's east wall is an open mouth into the great_room (no door — codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open). 1.2 m floor = two-person flow at the mouth
great_room + kitchen are one open LDK (gr style)

soft_proximities.note: keep the master-adjacent side of the wet core close to master

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_clustered_baths_hall
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Soft floors tiered by priority. auto_apply (always): kitchen width >= 1.8 m (above PD 1096's 1.5 m hard min, usable galley); hall clear width >= 0.95 m (above 0.90 m corridor hard min). preferred_apply (tried first, relaxed as a group on infeasibility): baths at 3.0 m² preferred-low AND kitchen at 2.0 m generous galley. Bath priority is implicit in this split — when preferred relaxes, kitchen never drops below 1.8 m, but baths can drop toward the 1.2 m² hard min if necessary.

notes: v2 catalog topology #2 HALL-VARIANT — 2BR / 2 baths CLUSTERED with a mid-band
HALLWAY tucked between master (front) and standard (rear) inside the bath
block. Floor band ~78-90 m² (the hall is small, but the wider middle band it
sits in pushes the natural footprint up from topology #2's 65-72 m² band).
Program: same clustered bath block (ensuite + common share a wet_core) and
same front-master / rear-standard stack as topology #2. The difference is a
hallway sized into the middle band, east of common and west of the great_room.
Master's door is on its north wall (opens into the hall). Standard's door is
on its south wall (opens into the hall). Common's east-wall door opens into
the hall. The hall's east wall is open to the great_room (no door — codebase
rule auto-casts hallway↔great_room to open_plan).
Layout intent (canonical, brief.carport_preference='right'):
Front: [master                              | great_room               ]
Mid:   [ensuite | common  | HALL            | great_room               ]
Rear:  [standard                            | kitchen                  ]
Stack constraint: the new ['master','hall','standard'] front-to-rear stack
forces the hall into the middle band between master and standard (NOT a
full-depth strip along the great_room's west wall — that was the v1 hall
geometry, which the user rejected as 'a long strip that goes from outer wall
to outer wall').
Hall sizing: ~1.0 m wide x ~2.0 m deep ≈ 2.0 m², inside the hallway hard
minimum (1.5 m²) and at the low end of the soft preferred range (2.5-6.0 m²).
The mid-band thickness is set by the bath block; with 2.0 m bath-block depth
the hall lands at ~2.0 m² — tight but a usable PH corridor.
Privacy gain over topology #2: bedrooms (master, standard) share NO walls
with the great_room — bedroom doors face the hall, not the LDK. Common bath
is no longer publicly accessible from the great_room; it's reached through
the hall.
Carport: 3.0 m wide x 6.0 m deep (Item 2 default). The 1 m x 4 m exceeding
the setbacks becomes a front-right building_void — same L-shape pattern as
topology #2.
When brief.carport_preference='left', the runner mirrors the topology so the
private wing flips to the right side.
